prompt: Translate this English proverb into two other languages in a way that preserves the spirit, not the words: 'The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now.' Add a one-sentence note on each.
response:
# Translations of "The best time to plant a tree was twenty years ago. The second best time is now."

## Spanish
**"Quien no sembró ayer, que tome la pala hoy; la tierra no pregunta por qué tardaste."**

*Note:* This version leans into Spanish proverbial style by personifying the earth, emphasizing that action—not excuses—is what the soil rewards.

## Japanese
**「昨日蒔かなかった種を悔やむより、今日の土を耕せ。」**
*(Kinō makanakatta tane o kuyamu yori, kyō no tsuchi o tagayase.)*

*Note:* Rooted in the Japanese sensibility of *ima koko* (here and now), this rendering contrasts regret over unsown seeds with the quiet dignity of tilling today's soil.
